title: The Life and Legacy of Annabel Schofield: Model, Actress, and Cancer Advocate
summary: Annabel Schofield, a Welsh-born model, producer, and actress, passed away at age 62 from cancer on February 28 in Los Angeles. Born in 1963 in Llanelli, she began her career at 16 by moving to London, where she modeled for top fashion houses like Versace and Yves Saint Laurent. She gained international fame in the late 1980s through a Bugle Boy jeans commercial, which featured her driving a Ferrari. Schofield later expanded into acting, appearing in the film Bloodtide and the TV series Dallas during its 11th season. She also worked as a film and TV producer and authored a semi-autobiographical novel. In her later years, she battled cancer, undergoing surgery in 2023 and chemotherapy in 2025 after it spread to her brain. She publicly discussed her diagnosis to raise funds for treatment. Melissa Richardson, her modeling agency owner, praised Schofield as a key figure in the agency's success, describing her as talented, loyal, and down-to-earth. Her father was movie producer John D. Schofield, adding to her entertainment industry connections. Schofield's story highlights a journey from Welsh roots to Hollywood, marked by professional achievements and personal resilience against illness.

background_read: Annabel Schofield was a multifaceted figure in entertainment, starting as a model in 1980s London and rising to fame through fashion and commercials. Her transition to acting in Dallas and producing roles showcased her versatility. Born into a film-producing family, she leveraged connections while building her own legacy. Her battle with cancer later in life brought attention to health advocacy, as she used her platform to seek financial support for treatment. This context helps understand her impact on fashion, television, and personal resilience.
